title: Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu (Hakîkî) Manuscript Witness Census

creators.name: Kazemipourleilabadi, Negar
creators.affiliation: Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München, Department of Art History

description:
A codicological inventory of manuscript witnesses to the Dīvān of Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 872 AH / 1467 CE; pen name Ḥaqīqī / Hakîkî), Qaraqoyunlu ruler-poet.

The census comprises 14 audited entries: 9 verified Jahanshah witnesses (5 on the TEIS Yesevi roster, 1 institutional fragment, 3 Alevi shrine witnesses in the Ocak tradition: 1 Anatolian (Diyarbakır Ulutürk) and 2 Iranian Azerbaijani (Ilkhchi Kırklar Ocağı)), 2 candidates flagged as probably non-Jahanshah, 1 lost-attested-only (Diyarbakır YE per Cunbur 1999), and 2 rejected. The 2 rejected entries are audit-preserved rebuttals to specific claims in published scholarship (the Rahimov 1986 Baku edition) and in a 2021 institutional acquisition announcement (AMEA Manuscripts Institute). Routine search false-positives are recorded separately in the per-session search JSONs under data/searches/, not in the register itself. Includes a 5-family search-key matrix (modern Latin, 19th-c. Orientalist, Soviet-Cyrillic, Arabic-script, catalogue-context) covering Persian, Turkish, Ottoman Turkish, and Azerbaijani Turkic forms; 5 tezkire entries plus 5 research-log extracts (verbatim sample verses where available).

The Alevi shrine corpus (Anatolian and Iranian Azerbaijani Ocak tradition) preserves eleven ghazals not present in the institutional editions examined, plus a complete unphotographed divan codex.

Methodology: Witness census combining aggregator sweeps (~30 union catalogues), tezkire/reference extraction (10 extract files: 5 tezkire entries plus 5 research-log extracts), mecmûʿa adjacency mining, auction-house archives, scribe-name search, printed-catalogue OCR backchain, and modern editor backchain.
